description: Lifecycle endpoints never mutate bodies (RFC-ACDP-0013 §6 step 2). A retract request that attempts to supply or alter body content MUST be rejected with the immutable_field error code (HTTP 400) — activated in 0.3.0 from the reservation held since v0.1.0 (RFC-ACDP-0007 §5, RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.1) — distinct from generic schema_violation so producers learn the category error. Also pins the endpoint authentication failures.

input.precondition: The sig-001 golden context, active, on a registry advertising acdp-registry-lifecycle.

scenarios.name: A — request carries a body member

expected.behavior: The 'body' member is an attempt to modify immutable body content through a lifecycle endpoint. The registry MUST reject with immutable_field (NOT schema_violation) and MUST NOT append any event or change any state.

scenarios.name: B — request carries a body-field-named member

expected.behavior: An envelope member named after a body field (title, summary, metadata, data_refs, ...) is the same category error as scenario A: immutable_field, no state change. (An unknown member NOT naming body content — e.g. 'note' — is a plain schema_violation against the closed request envelope; the distinction is what this fixture pins.)

scenarios.name: C — actor is not the producer

expected.behavior: event.actor != body.agent_id: rejected with not_authorized — the same authorization rule as supersession by a different agent_id (RFC-ACDP-0003 §3.1 step 3, RFC-ACDP-0013 §6 step 3). Visibility is checked FIRST (§6 step 1): a requester who could not retrieve the context gets not_found (404), never a distinguishable 403.

Producer-initiated events MUST be signed (RFC-ACDP-0013 §5); a missing signature fails the §6 step 2/3 validation. A present-but-unverifiable signature is invalid_signature instead; key-resolution failures map exactly as at publish time.

notes: immutable_field and invalid_lifecycle_transition (see lc-001 scenario C) are 0.3.0 wire codes: implementations declaring acdp_version < 0.3.0 MUST NOT emit them (registries/error-codes.md; acdp-error.schema.json enum).
